content: For 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems, we can hypothesize that structuring memory around open research question fragments (borrowed from how DeepResearch Agents track evolving knowledge) will deliver stronger curiosity-driven exploration performance than existing prediction-error based curiosity methods like ICM and RND. This memory schema prioritizes retaining unresolved, high-information-gain questions over just storing completed facts or observed transitions, driving agents to actively seek data that resolves their open knowledge gaps.
